meta.title: US House Control after 2026 Midterms
meta.question: How many House seats will Democrats hold after the November 2026 midterm elections?
meta.resolution: AP/major-network call following November 3 2026 election; certified results announced by mid-December 2026

priorJustifications.H1: Democratic landslide (≥226 seats) requires very strong thermostatic effect against unified R government plus R coalition stress; possible but not modal.
priorJustifications.H2: Democratic narrow flip (218-225) is modal — historical midterm pattern under unified R suggests +20-30 seat swing toward minority party.
priorJustifications.H3: Republican narrow hold (210-217 D seats) if economy holds and R turnout matches D enthusiasm.
priorJustifications.H4: Republican expansion (<210 D seats) requires major D collapse or unprecedented R coalition gains; tail risk.

redTeamGapsAddressed: H4 (R expand) lacks supporting source — historical base rate not anchored
FEC Q1 2026 DCCC/NRCC fundraising + candidate quality missing
Generic ballot single snapshot, no trend or house-effect adjustment
meta.priorRevisionRationale: 10-source corpus strongly supports D modal outcome. Source 5 (R retirement record 36 vs 22) is historical analog to 2018 when Dems won +40. Source 5 (generic ballot D+5.4 stable) implies 12-20 R seat losses. Source 1 special election overperformance massive (+12 to +25). Source 1 Trump approval ~39%. H4 base rate from Source 6 (historical) ~2-3% conditional on no security shock. H1 elevated to ~0.26 reflecting genuine wave-environment markers but constrained by competitive seat geography (only 17 toss-ups). H3 down to ~0.20 reflecting evidence environment heavily favors D. H4 to ~0.04 floor.
meta.lensSetReason: Initial lens set during topic creation per skills/news-scan.md mapping

issues: Unfalsifiable hypotheses: H1, H2, H3, H4

blockers: NON-UNIFORM PRIORS without justification — posteriors are {'H1': 0.26, 'H2': 0.5, 'H3': 0.2, 'H4': 0.04} but posteriorHistory has no initial note explaining the asymmetry. Add a posteriorHistory entry documenting why.
warnings: No anti-indicators defined — every hypothesis needs at least one indicator that would REDUCE its probability

_design_rules: Each indicator must be OBSERVABLE — actions, not opinions
Each must have a clear fired/not-fired threshold
Every hypothesis needs at least one pro AND one anti indicator
Governor flags unfalsifiable hypotheses as DEGRADED

tier1_critical.desc: RCP avg + at least ONE of {538/Silver Bulletin avg, NYT/Siena, CNN poll-of-polls} both showing D+8 or larger sustained 30 days within Sep-Oct 2026. Source: RCP + secondary aggregator (cross-source requirement to avoid RCP monoculture).
RCP avg + secondary aggregator both showing D+3 to D+7 band sustained 30 days Sep-Oct 2026 (modal D-flip but no landslide).
RCP avg + secondary aggregator both showing tied/D+0 to D+2 band sustained 30 days Sep-Oct 2026 (R-narrow-hold modal).
RCP avg + secondary aggregator both showing R+5 or larger sustained 30 days Sep-Oct 2026 (threshold raised from R+2 for D-skew symmetry).

Cumulative Dem overperformance (vs 2024 presidential margin) >=R+5 average across >=10 federal/state special elections held Jan 2026 - Aug 2026 (window capped at end-Aug to avoid resolution-adjacency with November). Source: Daily Kos / Split-Ticket.

tier2_strong.desc: RCP avg + at least ONE of {Gallup, Pew, Quinnipiac} both showing Trump approval below 40% sustained 60 days in 2026.
RCP avg in 42-46% Trump approval band sustained 60 days in 2026 (modal-environment H3 indicator). Source: RCP + cross-aggregator.
RCP avg + secondary showing Trump approval >=50% sustained 60 days in 2026.

NBER calls recession OR BEA prints 2 consecutive negative real GDP quarters before Nov 3 2026 — splits LR across H1 (severe-environment landslide) AND H2 (modal D flip). REV4: causal_event_id removed — mutually exclusive with t2_strong_economy_q3 (cannot both fire).

DCCC Q2+Q3 2026 cash-on-hand >= NRCC AND >=10 strong Dem recruits (per Cook/CR analysis) in R-held seats by Aug 2026 (campaign fundamentals favor narrow flip). Source: FEC filings + Cook Political Report.

Q3 2026 BEA advance real GDP >=3.0% SAAR AND U-3 unemployment <=4.0% in Sep 2026 BLS print (joint condition). RENAMED from anti_ in REV4 — this is pro-H4, not adversarial. causal_event_id removed in REV5 — mutually exclusive with t2_recession_called.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Cook Political Report 'Toss Up + Lean R' House seat count grows by >=10 between Jan 2026 and Oct 2026 ratings.
Cook Political Report 'Toss Up + Lean D' House seat count grows by >=10 between Jan 2026 and Oct 2026 ratings.

Net redistricting changes (court orders, mid-decade redraws) Jan-Oct 2026 yield D net pickup of >=3 seats relative to Jan 2026 map (LR moderated — partly mechanical). Source: Loyola Law / Brennan Center.

anti_indicators.desc: Cumulative Dem performance (vs 2024 margin) <=R-2 across >=10 federal/state specials Jan-Aug 2026 (genuine anti-H1 falsifier — H1 landslide thesis dies with under-performing specials). Source: Daily Kos / Split-Ticket.
BLS NFP avg >200k AND U-3 declining for Sep+Oct 2026 prints (genuine anti-H4 — late labor strength kills R-expansion thesis specifically). Source: BLS.
Major US-involved kinetic security incident (US casualties >=10 OR domestic mass-casualty terror attack) within 60 days before Nov 3 2026 (LR moderated — historical rally-round-flag effect is mixed).

DCCC Q2+Q3 2026 cash-on-hand <0.7x NRCC AND <5 strong Dem recruits (per Cook/CR analysis) in R-held seats by Aug 2026 (kills H2 narrow-flip thesis structurally — pushes mass to H3 R-narrow-hold). Source: FEC + Cook.

Net GOP House retirements (excluding R candidates retiring to seek other office) reach >=15 by Aug 2026 (historically high — open-seat effect favors Dem pickup, pushes mass from H3 to H1/H2). Source: Roll Call retirement tracker.

actorModel.description: House Democratic seat count in 2026 emerges from: national political environment (Trump approval, generic ballot), candidate-quality recruitment, redistricting maps, late-cycle shocks, and turnout asymmetries. Cook/Sabato ratings synthesize multiple signals; AP race calls resolve.

trump.name: Donald Trump (President, R brand)
trump.role: Single biggest determinant of national environment. Approval rating cascades into generic ballot, recruitment, retirements, and turnout.
trump.decisionStyle: Reactive, communication-heavy, disregards conventional political wisdom.
biases: Willingness to escalate cultural conflicts close to election
Tariff/immigration policy creates recurring news cycles
filters: Polling, base sentiment, Fox News framing
overrides: Major economic shock pre-election forces tactical messaging shift

dccc_nrcc.name: DCCC / NRCC (party committees)
dccc_nrcc.role: Recruitment quality and resource allocation. Strong recruits in target seats and fundraising parity are necessary conditions for D narrow flip (H2).
dccc_nrcc.decisionStyle: Strategic, money-following, defensive vs offensive bucket allocation.
biases: Cash chasing polls more than fundamentals
filters: FEC filings, candidate quality scores (Cook/CR), special election results
overrides: Major retirement wave forces emergency recruiting in safe seats

voters_swing.name: Swing/Independent Voters (~10-15% of electorate)
voters_swing.role: Marginal seats decided by voters disproportionately responsive to economic conditions and security shocks.
voters_swing.decisionStyle: Less ideologically anchored; respond to immediate conditions over narratives.
biases: Lower turnout when both parties unappealing
overrides: Security shock OR sharp recession reverses approval-to-vote translation

election_forecasters.name: Cook Political Report / Sabato / Inside Elections / 538
election_forecasters.role: Synthesize polling + fundamentals + district-level intelligence into ratings. Cook ratings drift across year is a high-signal observable.
election_forecasters.decisionStyle: Conservative on rating changes; lag fundamentals by 4-8 weeks.
biases: Slow to revise on a single wave of polls
filters: Generic ballot, special election overperformance, recruitment quality, cash on hand

ap_race_calls.name: AP / Decision Desk HQ / Network Decision Desks
ap_race_calls.role: Sole arbiter of seat-count resolution. Calls finalize H1/H2/H3/H4 bands.
ap_race_calls.decisionStyle: Slow, multi-source, data-driven; some races take days.
biases: Conservative on close races; willing to leave uncalled
filters: Vote tabulation, mail-in ballots, recount thresholds

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — Special-election votes are EVENT (highest weight); generic-ballot polls are DATA; political-pundit takes are RHETORIC.
GENERIC BALLOT D-SKEW — RCP genballot averages ~3pt D-favorable historically. R+2 generic ballot is roughly equivalent to a true tie environment, hence REV4 raised tier1 R-band threshold to R+5.

SPECIAL ELECTIONS LEAD — Cumulative special-election overperformance through August has historically correlated 0.6-0.7 with November result. Cap firing window at end-Aug to avoid resolution-adjacency.
POLLSTER MONOCULTURE GUARD — Require RCP + at least one named primary pollster (NYT/Siena, Gallup, Pew, Quinnipiac) on tier1 fires. RCP and 538 share underlying poll universe.
LATE-CYCLE SHOCK ASYMMETRY — Security shocks have mixed historical effects on incumbent party; LRs moderated.

rcp_genballot.label: RealClearPolitics generic-ballot polling average (D-vs-R House preference)
rcp_genballot.note: REV4 requires CROSS-AGGREGATOR confirmation from a primary pollster (NYT/Siena, Gallup, Pew, Quinnipiac) for tier1 fires. RCP+538 share underlying poll universe.

primary_pollsters.label: Primary pollsters: NYT/Siena, Gallup, Pew, Quinnipiac
primary_pollsters.note: Cross-aggregator validation source. Required on tier1 genballot fires (REV4).

rcp_trump_approval.label: RCP Trump job approval polling average
rcp_trump_approval.note: Three indicators key off 60-day sustained bands: <40 (H1/H2), 42-46 (H3), >=50 (H3/H4). Bound by causal_event_id=trump_approval_2026.

gallup_pew_quinnipiac_party_brand.label: Combined Gallup + Pew + Quinnipiac Democratic-party favorability
gallup_pew_quinnipiac_party_brand.note: Currently unused after REV4 (anti_d_primary_brand was dropped — too-extreme threshold).

specials_tracker.label: Special election overperformance tracker (Daily Kos, Split-Ticket)
specials_tracker.note: Cumulative D performance vs 2024 margin. REV4 caps firing window at end-Aug 2026 to avoid resolution-adjacency. anti_h1 (R-2 or worse) and tier1_dem_overperform pair.

cook_ratings.label: Cook Political Report House race ratings
cook_ratings.note: Toss-up + Lean shift counts (Jan vs Oct). Per_event_member with causal_event_id=cook_2026_oct_vs_jan.

fec_filings.label: FEC quarterly filings — DCCC/NRCC cash on hand + candidate fundraising

rollcall_retirement_tracker.label: Roll Call House retirement tracker
rollcall_retirement_tracker.note: anti_h3_gop_retirement_wave fires on >=15 net GOP retirements by Aug 2026.

redistricting_tracker.label: Loyola Law / Brennan Center redistricting tracker
redistricting_tracker.note: Net D pickup from redistricting Jan-Oct 2026.

bea_bls_economy_q3.label: BEA Q3 advance GDP + BLS Sep U-3 (cross-topic with recession)
bea_bls_economy_q3.note: t2_strong_economy_q3 (renamed from anti_ in REV4). Joint condition.

ap_decision_desk.label: AP / Decision Desk HQ House race calls
ap_decision_desk.note: Resolution arbiter. Some races take days to call; recount thresholds vary.
